lever: For accepted 3x3 batched Eigh slices, append the existing analytic solver's arrays directly and allocate generic scratch only when the analytic residual check rejects.

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Batch lane order and output order remain w then v; direct append uses the same [w0,w1,w2] and row-major [v00,v01,v02,...] arrays produced by analytic_eigh_3x3.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: Eigenvalue ordering and equal-value tie behavior stay inside analytic_eigh_3x3 or the unchanged Jacobi fallback; no new compare or sort rule is introduced.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Accepted 3x3 slices call the same analytic_eigh_3x3 function with the same nine f64 inputs. Rejected slices copy those same parsed f64 values into the unchanged generic decomposition fallback.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.error_paths: Shape, rank, non-square, batch-dim movement, tensor construction, and numeric type mismatch errors are preserved.

alien_inputs: Batch-structured small-matrix kernel: avoid generic per-slice materialization when a fixed-shape analytic primitive already returns the final dense outputs.
Communication-avoiding linear algebra principle applied at small scale: keep data on the stack and move only final outputs through the Value layer.
